retry_reason: The put operation always executes and never signals failure. If the refcount were already zero, calling this function would be a double-free bug; retrying would only worsen the corruption.
contract_summary: Release wrapper that decrements the reference count on a crypto_lskcipher tfm via crypto_free_lskcipher. After calling this function, the caller must not access the tfm pointer. The caller must ensure that the tfm is a valid, previously allocated object (non-NULL) and that a matching reference (e.g., from a prior get or allocation) exists. This function does not return a value and has no failure detection — any misuse (e.g., double put) is a caller bug.

judge.reason: The sole purpose of this function is to call crypto_free_lskcipher, a put function that decrements the reference count of the crypto_lskcipher tfm. There is no other logic — it is a pure release wrapper.
